title: Rich Japanese tofu ramen
description: Tangy, gooey ramen with tender steak and soft tofu, a nostalgic and comforting dish.
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add sliced steak, sear until browned on both sides, then remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add grated ginger and minced garlic, sauté until fragrant.
Pour in dashi broth, soy sauce, mirin, and rice vinegar, bring to a gentle boil.
Add shiitake mushrooms and simmer for 4 minutes.
Return steak to the skillet, let it steam for 2 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil ramen noodles in a separate pot for 3 minutes, then drain.
Stir in tofu cubes and sesame oil, cook for 1 minute until heated through.
Divide noodles into bowls, ladle broth over them, top with steak, tofu, sliced mushrooms, and spring onion.
Sprinkle chili flakes if desired, serve hot.
